mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal fails to predict direction with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which amplifies corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day returns in over 50% of observations during early-cycle expansions, or the autocorrelation-adjusted t-statistic remains below 1.8 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding a 0.3 percent threshold signals accelerating price pressures that compress real yields and trigger central bank tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 58% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue streams, which directly supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant directional bias following positive GDP prints.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index signals supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle contractions, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 48%.
